description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ubuntu:bionic Docker image and including the Code-Quality, Dependency-Scanning and License-Scanning templates. Define stages test, promotion, version, backmerge. In the test stage add a test job that runs the npm setup commands (apt-get update -yqq, apt-get install -yqq build-essential curl default-jdk, curl -sL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_10.x | bash -, apt-get install -yqq nodejs, npm --version, npm install --unsafe-perm=true) then executes "npm run lint:pkg" and "npm run unit", and stores npm-debug.log as an artifact for 1 month. In the backmerge stage add a backmerge job that runs the git setup commands (apt-get update -yqq, apt-get install -yqq git openssh-client, git --version, git config --global user.email "${GITLAB_USER_EMAIL}", git config --global user.name "${GITLAB_USER_NAME}", git remote set-url origin --push "git@${CI_SERVER_HOST}:${CI_PROJECT_PATH}", git remote -v, eval $(ssh-agent -s), chmod 700 "${CI_DEPLOY_KEY}", ssh-add "${CI_DEPLOY_KEY}", mkdir -p ~/.ssh, echo -e "Host ${CI_SERVER_HOST}\n\tStrictHostKeyChecking no" >> ~/.ssh/config, chmod 700 ~/.ssh) then checks out develop, pulls it, merges the current branch without fast‑forward using "git merge origin/${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH} --no-ff -m \"[ci] Backmerge ${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH} -> develop\"", and pushes to develop; it runs only on the master branch when the commit message matches /\[ci\]/. In the version stage add a patch job that runs the npm setup then the git setup, then runs "npm version patch --unsafe-perm=true -m \"[ci] Patch version %s\"", pushes the commit with "git push origin HEAD:${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH}", pushes tags with "git push --tags", sets the npm auth token with "npm config set //registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken ${NPM_TOKEN}" and publishes with "npm publish"; it runs only on master when the commit message does not contain [ci] and is allowed to fail. In the promotion stage add three manual jobs (promote_major, promote_minor, promote_patch) that each run the npm and git setup, checkout the branch ("git checkout ${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH}" and "git reset --hard ${CI_COMMIT_SHA}"), bump the version with "npm version major --unsafe-perm=true -m \"Major version %s\"" or "npm version minor --unsafe-perm=true -m \"Minor version %s\"" for the respective jobs, merge master with "git merge origin/master", push HEAD to master and push tags, and for major and minor also publish to npm as above; they run only on develop when the commit message does not contain [ci] and must be triggered manually.
